understanding.goal_understanding: The goal of KA59 is to complete all levels by placing every selectable piece onto a goal tile simultaneously. I needed to control multiple selectable pieces and move them onto '+'-marked cells to win each level.
understanding.mechanics_understanding: Movement shifts the currently selected piece by 3-pixel grid steps in the chosen direction. Some objects block movement completely, while others can be pushed. Walls may behave inconsistently, and some wall-like boundaries are impassable. CLICK is meant to switch control to another selectable piece at the given pixel coordinates, but in my session, CLICK repeatedly failed to change selection, often reporting 'Selected piece did not change position (blocked or action has no movement effect)'. It seemed CLICK required the currently selected piece to be in a specific state or the target piece to be unobstructed, but I could not determine the exact condition. The step budget per level was limited, and running out of steps caused a loss.

Agent explained goal and mechanics.

understanding.goal_understanding: The goal of KA59 is to complete all levels by placing every selectable piece onto a goal tile simultaneously. I needed to control multiple selectable pieces and move them onto '+'-marked cells to win each level.
understanding.mechanics_understanding: Movement shifts the currently selected piece by 3-pixel grid steps in the chosen direction. Some objects block movement completely, while others can be pushed. Walls may behave inconsistently, and some wall-like boundaries are impassable. CLICK is meant to switch control to another selectable piece at the given pixel coordinates, but in my session, CLICK repeatedly failed to change selection, often reporting 'Selected piece did not change position (blocked or action has no movement effect)'. It seemed CLICK required the currently selected piece to be in a specific state or the target piece to be unobstructed, but I could not determine the exact condition. The step budget per level was limited, and running out of steps caused a loss.
